inline_comments.message: This will add the new columns in the case of a fresh install, but in an upgrade scenario we need to alter the table

There is no upgrade scenario implemented in the onboarding init solution.
I could not find also a way to add a column only if it does not exist when using a cql script.

As a workaround, I've kind of activated the use of an alter_table.cql script (it was being added to the init docker image, but not being called), and added the alter tables there. 
The alter_table.cql result is being ignored, so it will not guarantee a successful upgrade. I believe if one cql command fails, the next ones will not be executed, and I also could not find an error handling in cql scripts.

I could, though, split every single command (3 for now) in a separate file and force the execution of all commands if wanted (ignoring the result).

I've implemented a way to add and run upgrade scripts during the onboarding init: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/sdc/+/123263.
For this particular change, I've added a new script to be executed: openecomp-be/tools/install/database/upgrade-scripts/20210729-dox.package_details.cql
